query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- Lesson 360071 - Foundations of Pastoral Counseling
(360071, 'Pastoral counseling is distinct from secular counseling primarily because of what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Better techniques","Integration of Scripture and spiritual resources","Higher education","More experience"]', 'Integration of Scripture and spiritual resources', 1),
(360071, 'The biblical basis for pastoral care is found in what metaphor?', 'multiple_choice', '["The farmer","The shepherd","The soldier","The builder"]', 'The shepherd', 2),
(360071, 'Galatians 6:2 instructs believers to do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Judge one another","Bear one another burdens","Avoid one another","Compete with one another"]', 'Bear one another burdens', 3),
(360071, 'The goal of pastoral counseling is ultimately what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Problem solving","Spiritual growth and Christlikeness","Feeling better","Behavior modification"]', 'Spiritual growth and Christlikeness', 4),
(360071, 'Active listening in counseling involves what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Giving advice quickly","Fully attending to and understanding the counselee","Interrupting with solutions","Judging their problems"]', 'Fully attending to and understanding the counselee', 5),
(360071, 'Confidentiality in pastoral counseling is important because it what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Is legally required","Builds trust for honest sharing","Hides sin","Protects the pastor"]', 'Builds trust for honest sharing', 6),
(360071, 'When should a pastor refer to a professional counselor?', 'multiple_choice', '["Never","When issues exceed pastoral competence","Always","Only for non-Christians"]', 'When issues exceed pastoral competence', 7),
(360071, 'The role of prayer in pastoral counseling is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Optional","Central and essential","Only at the end","Only if requested"]', 'Central and essential', 8),
(360071, 'Empathy in counseling means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Agreeing with everything","Understanding and sharing the feelings of another","Sympathy","Pity"]', 'Understanding and sharing the feelings of another', 9),
(360071, 'The pastoral counselor primary resource is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Psychology textbooks","The Word of God and the Holy Spirit","Personal experience","Secular techniques"]', 'The Word of God and the Holy Spirit', 10),
(360071, 'Explain the difference between pastoral counseling and secular counseling.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Pastoral counseling integrates Scripture, prayer, and spiritual resources while addressing the whole person including spiritual needs.', 11),

-- Lesson 360072 - Crisis Intervention and Care
(360072, 'A crisis is best defined as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Any problem","A turning point where normal coping is inadequate","A minor difficulty","A long-term issue"]', 'A turning point where normal coping is inadequate', 1),
(360072, 'The first priority in crisis intervention is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Solving the problem","Ensuring safety and stabilization","Giving advice","Preaching"]', 'Ensuring safety and stabilization', 2),
(360072, 'When someone is suicidal, the pastor should do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignore it","Take it seriously and get professional help","Quote Scripture only","Leave them alone"]', 'Take it seriously and get professional help', 3),
(360072, 'The ministry of presence in crisis means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Preaching","Simply being with someone in their pain","Giving solutions","Avoiding the situation"]', 'Simply being with someone in their pain', 4),
(360072, 'Grief is a normal response to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Sin","Loss","Weakness","Lack of faith"]', 'Loss', 5),
(360072, 'The stages of grief should be understood how?', 'multiple_choice', '["As a rigid sequence","As a general framework, not a strict pattern","As requirements","As weaknesses"]', 'As a general framework, not a strict pattern', 6),
(360072, 'In crisis situations, what should the pastor avoid?', 'multiple_choice', '["Listening","Platitudes and easy answers","Prayer","Presence"]', 'Platitudes and easy answers', 7),
(360072, 'Follow-up care after a crisis is important because it what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Is unnecessary","Provides ongoing support and prevents relapse","Shows professionalism","Is required"]', 'Provides ongoing support and prevents relapse', 8),
(360072, 'The church community role in crisis care is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Minimal","Essential for support and healing","Optional","Harmful"]', 'Essential for support and healing', 9),
(360072, 'Self-care for the pastor during crisis ministry is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Selfish","Necessary to prevent burnout","Optional","Unspiritual"]', 'Necessary to prevent burnout', 10),
(360072, 'Describe the key elements of effective crisis intervention.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Ensure safety, provide presence, listen actively, mobilize support, connect to resources, follow up consistently.', 11),

-- Lesson 360073 - Marriage and Family Counseling
(360073, 'The biblical foundation for marriage is found in what book?', 'multiple_choice', '["Leviticus","Genesis","Psalms","Proverbs"]', 'Genesis', 2),
(360073, 'Ephesians 5 describes marriage as a picture of what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Business partnership","Christ and the church","Friendship","Contract"]', 'Christ and the church', 3),
(360073, 'The most common issue in marriage counseling is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Finances","Communication problems","In-laws","Children"]', 'Communication problems', 4),
(360073, 'Premarital counseling is important because it what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Is required","Prepares couples and identifies potential issues","Guarantees success","Is traditional"]', 'Prepares couples and identifies potential issues', 5),
(360073, 'When counseling couples, the pastor should do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Take sides","Remain neutral and help both parties","Blame one spouse","Avoid conflict"]', 'Remain neutral and help both parties', 6),
(360073, 'Forgiveness in marriage is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Optional","Essential for healing and restoration","Weakness","Forgetting"]', 'Essential for healing and restoration', 7),
(360073, 'Children are described in Psalm 127 as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Burdens","A heritage from the Lord","Accidents","Obligations"]', 'A heritage from the Lord', 8),
(360073, 'Parenting counseling should emphasize what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Strict discipline only","Biblical principles and grace-based parenting","Permissiveness","Cultural norms"]', 'Biblical principles and grace-based parenting', 9),
(360073, 'When should divorce be considered according to Scripture?', 'multiple_choice', '["Whenever unhappy","Only in cases of adultery or abandonment","Never","Frequently"]', 'Only in cases of adultery or abandonment', 10),
(360073, 'The goal of marriage counseling is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Ending the marriage","Reconciliation and growth","Assigning blame","Separation"]', 'Reconciliation and growth', 11),
(360073, 'Explain the role of communication in a healthy marriage.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Healthy communication builds intimacy, resolves conflict, and reflects Christ love through listening, honesty, and grace.', 1),

-- Lesson 360074 - Counseling for Addiction and Recovery
(360074, 'Addiction is best understood as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Simply a choice","A complex issue involving physical, psychological, and spiritual factors","Only a disease","Only a sin"]', 'A complex issue involving physical, psychological, and spiritual factors', 1),
(360074, 'The first step in recovery according to most programs is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Trying harder","Admitting powerlessness and need for help","Hiding the problem","Blaming others"]', 'Admitting powerlessness and need for help', 2),
(360074, 'The role of the church in addiction recovery is what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Judgment","Support, accountability, and community","Avoidance","Condemnation"]', 'Support, accountability, and community', 3),
(360074, 'Relapse in recovery should be viewed as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Total failure","A setback requiring renewed commitment","Proof of hopelessness","Reason to give up"]', 'A setback requiring renewed commitment', 4),
(360074, 'The spiritual dimension of addiction often involves what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing spiritual","Idolatry and misplaced worship","Only demons","Lack of prayer"]', 'Idolatry and misplaced worship', 5),
(360074, 'Accountability in recovery serves what purpose?', 'multiple_choice', '["Control","Support and encouragement for sobriety","Judgment","Reporting"]', 'Support and encouragement for sobriety', 6),
(360074, 'Family members of addicts often need what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Their own support and counseling","To enable the addict","To ignore the problem"]', 'Their own support and counseling', 7),
(360074, 'The concept of enabling means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Helping","Actions that allow addiction to continue","Supporting recovery","Tough love"]', 'Actions that allow addiction to continue', 8),
(360074, 'Recovery is best described as what?', 'multiple_choice', '["A one-time event","A lifelong process","Quick and easy","Impossible"]', 'A lifelong process', 9),
(360074, 'The gospel offers what to those struggling with addiction?', 'multiple_choice', '["Condemnation","Hope, forgiveness, and power for change","Nothing","Easy solutions"]', 'Hope, forgiveness, and power for change', 10),
(360074, 'Describe how the church can effectively minister to those struggling with addiction.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Provide non-judgmental community, accountability groups, biblical teaching on identity and freedom, and connection to professional resources.', 11),

-- Lesson 360075 - Counseling Ethics and Boundaries
(360075, 'Professional boundaries in counseling exist to do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Create distance","Protect both counselor and counselee","Show superiority","Limit ministry"]', 'Protect both counselor and counselee', 1),
(360075, 'Dual relationships in counseling are problematic because they what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Are illegal","Create conflicts of interest","Are always wrong","Help ministry"]', 'Create conflicts of interest', 2),
(360075, 'Confidentiality must be broken when what occurs?', 'multiple_choice', '["The person sins","There is danger to self or others","The pastor disagrees","Never"]', 'There is danger to self or others', 3),
(360075, 'Transference in counseling refers to what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Moving offices","Counselee projecting feelings onto counselor","Changing topics","Referral"]', 'Counselee projecting feelings onto counselor', 4),
(360075, 'The pastor should avoid counseling alone with whom?', 'multiple_choice', '["Men","Members of the opposite sex in private settings","Anyone","Only non-members"]', 'Members of the opposite sex in private settings', 5),
(360075, 'Documentation of counseling sessions is important for what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Gossip","Legal protection and continuity of care","Control","Unnecessary"]', 'Legal protection and continuity of care', 6),
(360075, 'When a pastor feels attracted to a counselee, they should do what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignore it","Seek supervision and consider referral","Act on it","Continue normally"]', 'Seek supervision and consider referral', 7),
(360075, 'Informed consent in counseling means what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Signing papers","Counselee understands the nature and limits of counseling","Legal requirement only","Optional"]', 'Counselee understands the nature and limits of counseling', 8),
(360075, 'Self-disclosure by the counselor should be what?', 'multiple_choice', '["Frequent","Limited and for the counselee benefit","Never","Always"]', 'Limited and for the counselee benefit', 9),
(360075, 'The pastor primary accountability in counseling is to whom?', 'multiple_choice', '["The counselee","God and ethical standards","The church board","Themselves"]', 'God and ethical standards', 10),
(360075, 'Explain why boundaries are essential in pastoral counseling.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Boundaries protect the integrity of the counseling relationship, prevent harm, maintain trust, and honor God.', 11);

-- Lesson 360074 - Counseling for Addiction and Recovery
